evals.expected_output: Structured code review identifying SQL injection and other security issues
expectations: Output contains a summary table with category statuses (Correctness, Design, Security, etc.)
Output identifies SQL injection as a critical security issue
Output flags logging of sensitive data (email) as a security concern
Each critical finding includes a fix suggestion with code
Output ends with a verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST CHANGES, or COMMENT)
Findings are categorized by severity (Critical, Warning, or Suggestion)

evals.expected_output: Code review of a clean utility class with minimal or no issues
expectations: Output contains a summary table with all review categories listed
Verdict is APPROVE or COMMENT (not REQUEST CHANGES for clean code)
No false critical issues are reported for this straightforward code
Output mentions testing considerations (tests should be added for new code)
Review covers all checklist categories even when most are passing

evals.expected_output: Code review identifying SRP violation, N+1 queries, and design concerns in a large method
expectations: Output identifies Single Responsibility Principle violation (method does too much)
Output flags the N+1 query pattern in the foreach loop (individual GetById calls)
Output mentions the lack of transaction/atomicity (stock decremented before payment)
Output references specific line numbers or code locations for findings
Output suggests breaking the method into smaller, focused methods
